Developer ToolsBeta

Canvas

TypeScriptWebGLWASM

Visual programming environment for complex system design. Enables teams to model, simulate, and deploy distributed architectures.

The Problem

Distributed system design lives in whiteboards, documentation, and tribal knowledge. There's no single tool that lets you move from architecture diagram to runnable simulation to deployment configuration.

What We Built

Canvas uses WebGL for its rendering layer, enabling smooth manipulation of diagrams with thousands of nodes. The simulation engine runs in WebAssembly, giving near-native performance for complex system modeling directly in the browser.

Teams can define services, data flows, failure modes, and scaling policies visually — then export to Terraform, Kubernetes manifests, or Pulumi configs.

Current State

Canvas is in beta with several design partners. We're currently iterating on the simulation engine and refining the export pipeline based on feedback from production engineering teams.

Status

Beta
